2605.03882.title: Deco: Extending Personal Physical Objects into Pervasive AI Companion through a Dual-Embodiment Framework
2605.03882.paper_summary: Individuals frequently form deep attachments to physical objects (e.g., plush toys) that usually cannot sense or respond to their emotions. While AI companions offer responsiveness and personalization, they exist independently of these physical objects and lack an ongoing connection to them. To bridge this gap, we conducted a formative study (N=9) to explore how digital agents could inherit and extend the emotional bond, deriving four design principles (Faithful Identity, Calibrated Agency, Ambient Presence, and Reciprocal Memory). We then present the Dual-Embodiment Companion Framework, instantiated as Deco, a mobile system integrating multimodal Large Language Models (LLMs) and Augmented Reality to create synchronized digital embodiments of users' physical companions. A within-subjects study (N=25) showed Deco significantly outperformed a personalized LLM-empowered digital companion baseline on perceived companionship, emotional bond, and design-principle scales (all p<0.01). A seven-day field deployment (N=17) showed sustained engagement, subjective well-being improvement (p=.040), and three key relational patterns: digital activities retroactively vitalized physical objects, bond deepening was driven by emotional engagement depth rather than interaction frequency, and users sustained bonds while actively navigating digital companions' AI nature. This work highlights a promising alternative for designing digital companions: moving from creating new relationships to dual embodiment, where digital agents seamlessly extend the emotional history of physical objects.
2605.03882.paper_summary_zh: 個體經常對無法感知或回應其情感的實體物品（例如，毛絨玩具）形成深厚的依附。雖然AI伴侶提供了反應性和個性化，但它們獨立於這些實體物品，並且缺乏與它們的持續連結。為了彌補這一差距，我們進行了一項形成性研究（N=9），探討數位代理如何繼承和擴展情感聯結，並得出了四個設計原則（忠實身份、校準代理、環境存在和互惠記憶）。接著，我們提出了雙重具身伴侶框架，以Deco為實例，這是一個整合多模態大型語言模型（LLMs）和擴增實境的移動系統，旨在創造用戶實體伴侶的同步數位具身。內部受試者研究（N=25）顯示，Deco在感知伴侶關係、情感聯結和設計原則量表上顯著優於個性化的LLM驅動數位伴侶基準（所有p<0.01）。為期七天的實地部署（N=17）顯示出持續的參與、主觀幸福感的改善（p=.040），以及三個關鍵的關係模式：數位活動追溯性地振興了實體物品，聯結加深是由情感參與的深度驅動，而非互動頻率，並且用戶在主動導航數位伴侶的AI特性時保持了聯結。這項工作突顯了設計數位伴侶的一個有前景的替代方案：從創造新關係轉向雙重具身，讓數位代理無縫延伸實體物品的情感歷史。
2605.03882.author: Zhihan Jiang et.al.
2605.03882.authors: Zhihan Jiang, Mengyuan Millie Wu, Ruishi Zou, Shiyu Xu, Xun Qian, Emma Macmanus, Steven Liao, Ping Zhang, Bingsheng Yao, Tingyu Cheng, James L. David, Nabila El-Bassel, Lena Mamykina, Frances R. Levin, Ryan Sultan, Dakuo Wang, Xuhai Xu
